circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281-kilometer track designed by Hermann Tilke on Abu Dhabi's Yas Island, has been the venue for Formula One's season-closing Abu Dhabi Grand Prix annually since 2009. It represents the second of four Middle Eastern Formula One circuits, following Bahrain and preceding those built in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: Albert Park Circuit is a street track that loops around a lake in Melbourne's Albert Park suburb and hosts the Australian Grand Prix as part of Formula One's annual calendar, alongside various supporting motorsport events. The venue maintains the highest FIA safety and facility standards required for international motorsport competition.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit originally operated as the Österreichring and was the venue for the Austrian Grand Prix throughout the 1970s and 1980s. After undergoing modifications and being renamed, the track returned as a Grand Prix host from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street-based racing venue positioned along Baku Boulevard in Azerbaijan's capital, spanning 6. 003 km per lap and ranking as the fourth-longest track in Formula One. The circuit first hosted international grand prix racing in 2016 with the European Grand Prix, before becoming home to the Azerbaijan Grand Prix from 2017 onwards.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, Bahrain International Circuit is a 5. 412 km venue hosting the Formula One Bahrain Grand Prix, which marked the championship's inaugural Middle Eastern race. The FIA Grade 1 licensed facility accommodates various motorsports including GP2 Series and endurance racing, while featuring multiple circuit configurations.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, Spain, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it representative of modern racing demands. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstands.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: This Belgian circuit near Spa stretches 7. 004 kilometers and has hosted the Formula One Belgian Grand Prix continuously since 1985, with only two exceptions in 2003 and 2006. The venue's racing heritage dates back to 1925, and it was among the original circuits featured during the inaugural 1950 Formula One season.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island in Montreal, the Circuit Gilles Villeneuve is a 4. 361-kilometer temporary track that serves as the home of Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. Beyond its role as a premier single-seater venue, the circuit has also accommodated various international racing competitions including sports car championships and North American touring series.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Located in Shanghai's Jiading District, the Shanghai International Circuit is a motorsport venue primarily recognized for hosting the Chinese Grand Prix, which took place annually from 2004 to 2019 before resuming in 2024. The track additionally began hosting the Shanghai ePrix electric racing event starting in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located near villages in Northamptonshire, England, Silverstone Circuit has been the traditional venue for the British Grand Prix since 1948 and notably hosted the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After sharing hosting duties with other circuits between 1955 and 1986, the British Grand Prix became a permanent fixture at Silverstone from 1987 onwards.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, situated near Budapest in Mogyoród, hosted the inaugural Formula One Grand Prix held within the Eastern Bloc in 1986, marking a significant expansion of the championship into previously untapped territory. Built in just eight months starting from late 1985, the 4. 381 km circuit was constructed outside the capital near a major highway after government officials rejected plans for a Monaco-style street venue in Budapest's central park.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: Located near Milan in northern Italy, the Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km circuit constructed in 1922 and recognized as Europe's oldest purpose-built racing venue. The track has continuously hosted the Italian Grand Prix since 1949, earning the nickname "Temple of Speed" for its characteristic long straights and fast-cornering layout.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit is a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport venue in Mie Prefecture, operated by Honda Mobilityland and accommodating 155,000 spectators. The track primarily hosts Formula One and Japanese Super Formula competitions, serving as one of motorsport's most prestigious racing destinations.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, adjacent to Las Vegas, and follows portions of the famous Las Vegas Boulevard. The track passes by iconic destinations such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, incorporating the Strip's renowned hotel and casino district into its routing.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: Located in Mexico City, the Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez is a 4. 304 km circuit honoring brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both killed in racing accidents in the 1960s and 1970s. The track has hosted Formula One's Mexican Grand Prix multiple times across different layouts, most recently as a regular fixture since 2015 under the Mexico City Grand Prix designation.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: Miami's temporary circuit winds around Hard Rock Stadium, spanning 3. 363 miles with 19 corners and generating qualifying speeds near 139 mph through its design by specialist track architects. The venue joined the Formula One calendar in 2022 as the home of the Miami Grand Prix.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street course that winds through the Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts of Monaco, encircling the harbour area. Though officially named after the circuit itself, it is widely known as "Monte Carlo" since the majority of its layout traverses the Monte Carlo neighbourhood.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes near Amsterdam, Zandvoort is a 4. 259 km circuit that resumed hosting Formula One racing in 2021 with the revival of the Dutch Grand Prix. The venue will cease its Formula One partnership following the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Lusail International Circuit is a Qatari racing venue situated north of Doha, measuring 5. 419 kilometers with a 1. 068-kilometer main straight and holding FIA Grade 1 certification.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km Brazilian racing venue situated in São Paulo that opened in 1940. The track was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace following his fatal aviation accident eight years earlier, and now serves as his burial site.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street circuit positioned along Saudi Arabia's Red Sea coastline that has served as the venue for the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its debut in 2021. The track also began hosting a Formula E race in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's waterfront district, incorporating the Downtown Core and Kallang planning areas into its layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, Spain, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile test for Formula One teams. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that hosts multiple racing series, most notably the Formula One United States Grand Prix alongside NASCAR and MotoGP events. Beyond F1, the track has accommodated various motorsport competitions including the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race and previously hosted IndyCar and other international racing championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Alexander Albon is a Thai-British driver currently racing for Williams in Formula One, competing under Thailand's flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: A Spanish driver currently racing for Aston Martin, Fernando Alonso is a two-time Formula One World Champion who claimed his titles in 2005 and 2006 during his Renault years, accumulating 32 race victories over 23 seasons. Beyond single-seaters, he has achieved remarkable success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans victories and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the sole driver to win both F1 and World Endurance championships.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Andrea Kimi Antonelli, an Italian driver, made his Formula One debut in 2025 and currently races for Mercedes. He has secured five Grand Prix victories since entering the sport.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas in the championship.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian racing driver Gabi Bortoleto currently competes in Formula One driving for Audi, bringing his racing expertise to the German manufacturer's team.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas has secured multiple podium finishes in Formula One's world championship, most notably finishing second in consecutive seasons with Mercedes. Currently representing Cadillac, Bottas has accumulated ten race victories throughout his lengthy career spanning over a decade.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Alejandro Colapinto is an Argentine motorsport competitor currently driving in Formula One's top championship series with Alpine.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Australian driver Jack Doohan combines endurance racing in Europe's LMP2 category with a reserve driver role at Haas, having made seven Formula One starts during the 2024-2025 season.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The 2020 Italian Grand Prix victory with AlphaTauri remains a significant achievement in his career.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Hadjar is a Franco-Algerian driver competing in Formula One for Red Bull Racing under French nationality.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari who has secured seven world championship titles, equalling Michael Schumacher's record. The veteran driver holds multiple circuit records including the most race victories, pole positions, and podium finishes in the sport's history.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: Nicolas Hülkenberg is a German racing driver currently competing in Formula One for Audi. Beyond his Formula One career, he achieved significant success in endurance racing, notably winning the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson is a New Zealand-born racing driver currently competing in Formula One with Racing Bulls.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: Charles Leclerc, a Monégasque driver competing for Ferrari, finished second in the 2022 drivers' championship and has claimed nine race victories throughout his nine seasons in Formula One.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen is a Danish driver competing in endurance racing with BMW through WRT and part-time in NASCAR with Trackhouse Racing. He accumulated two separate spells in Formula One, racing from 2014-2020 and returning for the 2022-2024 seasons.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver currently racing for McLaren, claimed the 2025 Formula One World Drivers' Championship with the team. He has accumulated 11 Grand Prix victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. The driver achieved a Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian round while competing for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Pérez, a Mexican driver, currently races for Cadillac in Formula One after spending 15 seasons in the sport. He finished second in the 2023 drivers' championship while driving for Red Bull and has accumulated six race victories throughout his career.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri, an Australian driver, currently races for McLaren in Formula One and has accumulated nine Grand Prix victories over his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known by the nickname "the Honey Badger," competed in Formula One over a 14-year span from 2011 until 2024, securing victory in eight Grand Prix races during his career.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell competes for Mercedes in Formula One, where he has claimed seven Grand Prix victories during his eight-year career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes for Williams in Formula One, where he has secured four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: Logan Sargeant is an American driver who raced in Formula One during 2023 and 2024 before transitioning to international sports car racing. He currently competes in the FIA World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition, following prior involvement in IMSA's LMP2 class.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One driver currently racing for Aston Martin in the sport's premier championship.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Japanese driver Yuki Tsunoda held a reserve position with Red Bull Racing and its sister team Racing Bulls during his Formula One tenure from 2021 through 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch racing driver competes for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive world championship titles between 2021 and 2024. Across his 12 seasons in the sport, he has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu, China's sole Formula One competitor to date, raced in the championship between 2022 and 2024 before transitioning to a reserve role with Cadillac. He currently holds that backup position within the team's Formula One programme.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine Racing operates from its Enstone base as Renault's works Formula One team, having been rebranded from Renault F1 Team in 2021 to represent the French manufacturer's Alpine sports car division before Renault's departure at the end of 2025. The outfit has claimed a single victory in its Alpine identity, courtesy of Esteban Ocon's triumph at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix, and currently competes with Mercedes power units.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin has entered Formula One racing through multiple incarnations as a British manufacturer, with the current iteration operating as AMR GP Limited under the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team identity.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari stands as Formula One's most enduring and accomplished team, participating continuously in the championship since its inception in 1950. The Italian outfit, identifiable by their iconic prancing horse emblem, represents the racing operations of Ferrari and remains the most victorious competitor in the sport's history.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: An American Formula One outfit founded by NASCAR team owner Gene Haas debuted in 2016, operating as a competitive midfield entrant throughout its existence. The team is currently led by principal Ayao Komatsu, following the departure of Guenther Steiner who held the position since the team's establishment through early 2024.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Swiss-based Sauber Motorsport was established in 1970 and initially gained prominence through endurance racing, winning the 1989 Le Mans 24 Hours with Mercedes-Benz. The team competed in Formula One from 1993 until 2025, becoming the fourth-longest-running constructor by race entries before being acquired by Audi AG to serve as the foundation for Audi's factory F1 program.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that operates as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group, which is owned by Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund Mumtalakat.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has competed in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and engine supplier, with its current team based in Brackley and competing under a German licence. The Mercedes-AMG Petronas operation became jointly owned by Mercedes-Benz Group, Ineos, and Toto Wolff in January 2022, with equal one-third stakes. The marque's racing teams are traditionally known as the "Silver Arrows".

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian-based Formula One constructor that entered the sport in 2024 as the second team owned by Red Bull GmbH, alongside Red Bull Racing. Operating from Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the team represents Red Bull's dual-team strategy in the championship.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian-based Formula One constructor that commenced competition in 2024 as part of Red Bull's two-team structure. Operating from Faenza with a secondary facility in Milton Keynes, the squad functions as a sister outfit to Red Bull Racing within the Austrian conglomerate's racing division.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian licence despite being English-based, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and has become one of two racing divisions within the Red Bull GmbH conglomerate. The team was led by Christian Horner throughout its two-decade tenure before his 2025 departure, with Laurent Mekies assuming leadership responsibilities.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering, a British Formula One constructor headquartered in Grove, Oxfordshire, was established in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head following the collapse of Williams's previous racing venture. The team will compete under the Atlassian Williams F1 Team name in 2026.
